blocks.content: Session Two. Family of Origin.

blocks.speaker: Dr. Reeves
blocks.content: Before we can talk about the marriage you're building, I need to understand the homes you came from. Not to diagnose anything — to understand what you're each carrying in. Daisy, let's start with you.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: My parents have been married for thirty years. My dad works in insurance. My mom taught elementary school. They fight sometimes but they always come back to each other. It was — stable. Safe. I knew what my house was going to feel like when I walked in.

blocks.speaker: Dr. Reeves
blocks.content: What did you learn about commitment from watching them.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: That it's not always easy but it's always a choice. My mom used to say love is a verb. You do it every day, whether you feel like it or not.

blocks.speaker: Dr. Reeves
blocks.content: Good. Hold that.
She turned to Kit.

blocks.speaker: Dr. Reeves
blocks.content: Kit.
He was quiet for a moment. The kind of quiet that had weight.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: My parents aren't together. My dad left when I was eleven.

blocks.speaker: Dr. Reeves
blocks.content: What does left mean. In your experience of it.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: He cheated on my mom. And then he left. One morning he was just — not there anymore. There was no conversation. No real explanation. Just a choice he made that ended everything.
The room — both rooms, two thousand miles apart — was very quiet.

blocks.speaker: Dr. Reeves
blocks.content: How old were you.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Eleven.

blocks.speaker: Dr. Reeves
blocks.content: What did that teach you about commitment.
He looked at his hands.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: That it can break. That someone can look you in the eye every day for years and still leave. That love isn't the same thing as staying.

blocks.speaker: Dr. Reeves
blocks.content: And what has that fear done to how you love people.
A long pause.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Made me careful. Made me slow. Made me — scared to name things because if you don't name them you can't lose them.

blocks.speaker: Dr. Reeves
blocks.content: And now you're naming the biggest thing there is.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Yeah.

blocks.speaker: Dr. Reeves
blocks.content: That took courage. I want you to hear that.
